sanskrit: सहस्रयुगपर्यन्तमहर्यद्ब्रह्मणो विदुः । रात्रिं युगसहस्रान्तां तेऽहोरात्रविदो जनाः ॥
hindiTranslation: जो मनुष्य ब्रह्माके एक हज़ार चतुर्युगीवाले एक दिनको और सहस्त्र चतुर्युगीपर्यन्त एक रातको जानते हैं, वे मनुष्य ब्रह्माके दिन और रातको जाननेवाले हैं ॥
englishTranslation: Those who know that Brahma’s day lasts a thousand yugas, and his night lasts a thousand yugas, know day and night truly.
essence: Cosmic time reveals how small ordinary urgency is.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Some people understand the vast scale of Brahma's day and night. They can see time on a cosmic scale, not just as ordinary human hours.
simpleInsight: A wider view can calm the mind that feels trapped by immediate time.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, with Arjuna shaken and the war still paused, Krishna turns from the soul’s freedom to the scale of cosmic time. He describes Brahma’s immense day and night to prepare Arjuna for a teaching about creation, dissolution, and what lies beyond them.
modernRelevance: You watch a deadline panic swallow the whole week, then remember the project is one cycle inside a much larger life. The pressure loosens when time stops feeling like a cage.
translationLiteral: सहस्र-युग-पर्यन्तम् (lasting a thousand yugas) / अहः (day) / यत् (which) / ब्रह्मणः (of Brahma) / विदुः (they know) / रात्रिम् (night) / युग-सहस्र-अन्ताम् (ending in a thousand yugas) / ते (they) / अहः-रात्र-विदः (knowers of day and night) / जनाः (people)
